_comment: Defines: (1) the exact output schema — only output_fields appear in canonical/company/ Parquet,
(2) per-field winner rules when Salesforce and NetSuite disagree.
Source IDs used in source_priority must match the source_id values in entity extraction config:

attribute_rules._comment: Legal / trading name. NetSuite is the system of record for the registered company name.
Primary billing email. NetSuite owns the billing relationship.
Phone. NetSuite wins; Salesforce may hold a sales contact number.
Annual revenue is a Salesforce-reported figure (CRM opportunity data). NetSuite does not store this.
Employee count is CRM data from Salesforce.
Credit limit is set by finance in NetSuite.
Outstanding balance is a live financial figure from NetSuite AR.
Currency of the billing relationship. NetSuite is authoritative.
Billing country from NetSuite (legal address); Salesforce may have the sales office address.
Billing state. Same rationale as billing_country.
Industry classification. Salesforce CRM is the system of record for market segmentation.
Active status. A company is active if either source marks it active.
Earliest created_date across systems — company may have existed in NetSuite before Salesforce.
Most recently updated record wins — ensures freshest modification timestamp.
